title: Fix immediate app-load crash causing ErrorBoundary fallback screen

requirements.text: Fix the app’s immediate-on-open crash that triggers the ErrorBoundary fallback screen ("Something unexpected happened") so the landing page reliably renders on first load.

quotes: theres an issue in loading the page , fix it

acceptanceCriteria: Opening the app in a fresh tab (no prior navigation) renders the landing page instead of the ErrorBoundary fallback.
Hard-refresh (Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+R) does not crash on initial render.
The fix specifically prevents runtime exceptions during initial navigation/history state initialization.

requirements.text: Harden browser history state synchronization in useExperienceNavigationState so it never throws when window.history.state is null or a non-object, and it preserves any existing history.state fields when adding the mindvault_experience flag.

quotes: App fails to load immediately on open; shows generic error: "Something unexpected happened... We encountered an issue loading this page."

acceptanceCriteria: When window.history.state is null/undefined, the hook initializes without throwing and writes a valid state via replaceState.
When window.history.state is an object, the hook keeps existing keys and only adds/updates the mindvault_experience key.
Open/close cycles of the Experience view still work (openExperience sets state true; closeExperience returns to landing) without crashing.
Back/forward navigation updates isExperienceOpen correctly without throwing.

requirements.text: Improve crash diagnostics when the ErrorBoundary is hit by surfacing the underlying runtime error details in the UI in a non-intrusive way while keeping the existing user-facing message text in English and unchanged in meaning.

quotes: We encountered an issue loading this page. This isn't your fault—please try again.

acceptanceCriteria: The ErrorBoundary fallback still shows the same headline, body copy, and "Try Again" button text.
The fallback provides an optional way to view technical details (at minimum: error name/message and a short stack excerpt if available).
Technical details are not shown by default; users must expand/reveal them.
Error details shown match the actual thrown error captured by the ErrorBoundary state.

constraints: Do not edit files in frontend immutablePaths (frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui).
Keep all user-facing text in English.
Backend remains single-actor Motoko; do not introduce additional backend services.
nonGoals: Adding new product features beyond fixing the load crash and improving related diagnostics.
Changing authentication flows (Internet Identity or Local Auth) unless required to resolve the crash.
Redesigning the landing page or Experience UI visuals beyond what is needed to fix the error state and diagnostics.
